title: impatiens tinctoria subsp. abyssinica
intro: Impatiens tinctoria subsp. abyssinica, also known as the Ethiopian balsam, is a plant species native to the highlands of Ethiopia and used for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Impatiens tinctoria subsp. abyssinica include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and aiding in the removal of mucus from the respiratory tract. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be made by boiling the leaves and stems in water or mixing the extracts with a carrier oil.
